config.weight_quant_body: INT4 g=64 (704 and 2112 not divisible by 128; H=2816/128=22 OK for some FCs)

notes: Gemma4 MoE uses 'parallel dense + sparse MoE' architecture: dense MLP and MoE run in parallel on same input, outputs combined after separate post-layernorms.
Unlike Qwen3.5-MoE, there is NO shared expert. Dense MLP serves a similar role.
Full attention layers use attention_k_eq_v=true: V=K, no separate V projection.
Full attention uses global_head_dim=512, partial_rotary_factor=0.25 (128 out of 512 dims rotated).
Sliding attention uses head_dim=256 with sliding_window=1024.
MoE expert activation is gelu_pytorch_tanh (GEGLU), not SiLU (SwiGLU).
MoE expert weights use INT4 g=64 (I_MOE=704, 704%128!=0). Dense MLP gate/up use g=128 (K=2816), down uses g=64 (K=2112).
LM_Head is tied with embeddings (tie_word_embeddings=true), uses INT8 g=128.

paged_attention_sliding.config: NH=16, NKV=8, HD=256, GQA=2, kv_int8, sliding_window=1024
paged_attention_sliding.note: Sliding window limits KV length to max 1024 tokens for decode

paged_attention_full.note: Full attention without sliding window, V=K due to k_eq_v

dense_mlp_gate_up.note: gate(2112) + up(2112) = 4224; GEGLU pattern; all 30 layers

moe_3gemm_fused.note: 128 experts, top_k=8, moe_intermediate_size=704, GEGLU activation, no shared expert

lm_head.note: tied with embeddings; only decode single token

small_ops_per_layer: rmsnorm (input_layernorm, post_attention_layernorm, pre_feedforward_layernorm, post_feedforward_layernorm, post_feedforward_layernorm_1, post_feedforward_layernorm_2, pre_feedforward_layernorm_2) x7 per MoE layer
